396 LOC · imports: context, loop, messages, models, prompts, renderer, runner, session, tools
git-isolated state machine · worktree lifecycle · delivery (pr/merge/output/none)
411 LOC · no REPL, no JSONL persistence
dim · bold · green · red · yellow · cyan
handles every AgentEvent branch — exhaustive match
collapses the agent_loop drain pattern in both roots
on_event hook lets caller observe events without owning the loop
agent_loop() async-generator · run_agent() drain helper · AgentConfig · AgentResult · make_config(cwd, …)

FakeProvider in tests/fake_provider.py satisfies this Protocol

handles thinking blocks · tool_use · cache_control · prompt caching
used by /model slash command, AgentConfig, AnthropicProvider
AgentEvent flow · one stream, two consumers
loop.agent_loop() yields → runner.drive() drains in order → on_event callback observes (renderer.render_event prints, autonomous logs) → AgentResult returned to root.

12 event types: ThinkingDeltaEvent · TextDeltaEvent · ToolCallEvent · ToolResultEvent · UsageEvent · PressureEvent · CompactionEvent · DoneEvent · DoneAgentEvent · AbortedAgentEvent · ErrorEvent · RetryEvent.
Invariants: turn-completeness (no partial assistant on error) · abort-completeness (placeholder ToolResult for unrun calls) · single exit via DoneAgentEvent.
drive() never raises — chat catches result.error · autonomous transitions FlowState on error.

Lower layers must never import from higher layers. Within a layer, modules may not cycle. L0 (messages, models) has zero internal imports. L1 wraps the SDK behind a Protocol. L2 modules depend only on L0/L1 and at most each other intra-layer (context → system_prompt only).

L3 (loop) is the sole orchestrator — yields AgentEvent, runs tools, streams from provider, hosts the Hooks seam. L4 wraps L3 for callers (drive · render · build prompts). L5 composes everything into chat (REPL) and autonomous (git-isolated pipeline).
See AGENTS.md for the full Concept Index, Change Impact Map, and Policies table.
